question: A process has a parallel (AND) gateway splitting into three outgoing sequence flows, joined later by a second parallel gateway. Which statement about this construct in Camunda 8.8 is NOT true?

options.text: Entering the split gateway causes all three outgoing sequence flows to be taken concurrently.
The join gateway waits until each of its incoming sequence flows has been taken before continuing.
The concurrent paths run as concurrent tokens on the same Zeebe partition, not as parallel OS threads.
The split gateway evaluates each outgoing flow's condition expression and takes only those whose condition is `true`.

a.text: True. When a parallel gateway with multiple outgoing sequence flows is entered, all flows are taken and the resulting paths execute concurrently and independently.

b.text: True. A parallel join waits at the gateway until each incoming sequence flow has been taken before the instance continues.

c.text: True. The docs note that outgoing paths of a parallel gateway are concurrent rather than parallel in the sense of OS threads; all records of a process instance are written to the same partition.

d.text: False. Parallel gateways do not evaluate conditions on their outgoing flows. Conditional routing is the job of exclusive or inclusive gateways; a parallel gateway always activates all outgoing flows.

explanation: A parallel (AND) gateway unconditionally splits the flow into all of its outgoing sequence flows and joins by waiting until each incoming flow has produced a token. It never inspects condition expressions on its outgoing flows — that is the role of exclusive and inclusive gateways. The concurrency is logical: records run on a single Zeebe partition, not on separate OS threads.
